Sure thing, Participants! I think capital punishment should be restricted to certain crimes or circumstances. First off, it seems pretty clear that some crimes deserve harsher punishments than others. For example, if someone commits premeditated murder, then they probably shouldn’t get the same sentence as someone who committed manslaughter by accident. It just doesn’t seem fair. Secondly, there are also situations where a person might have been wrongfully convicted or their trial was flawed in some way. In those cases, allowing for life without parole would mean that an innocent person could spend the rest of their life behind bars. That’s something we need to avoid at all costs.
